use std::thread;

fn process_items(items: Vec<String>) {
    let mut handles = Vec::new();
    for item in items {
        let handle = thread::spawn(move || {
            process_one(&item);
        });
        handles.push(handle);
    }
    for handle in handles {
        handle.join().unwrap();
    }
}

fn scan_all_targets(targets: Vec<String>) {
    for target in targets {
        thread::spawn(move || {
            scan(&target);
        });
    }
}

fn parallel_map(items: Vec<u64>) -> Vec<u64> {
    let mut handles = Vec::new();
    for item in items {
        handles.push(thread::spawn(move || item * 2));
    }
    handles.into_iter().map(|h| h.join().unwrap()).collect()
}

fn process_one(_item: &str) {}
fn scan(_target: &str) {}
